questions.question: What is meant by health?
questions.correct_answer: A state of complete physical, mental, and social well-being
questions.explanation: Health is not just the absence of disease but also includes mental and social well-being.

questions.question: What are the three main aspects of health?
questions.explanation: A healthy life requires balance between body, mind, and relationships.

questions.question: What are symptoms of a disease?
questions.correct_answer: Feelings experienced by a patient indicating illness
questions.explanation: Examples include pain, tiredness, or dizziness.

questions.question: What are signs of a disease?
questions.correct_answer: Observable or measurable indicators of illness
questions.explanation: Examples include fever, swelling, or rash.

questions.question: What are communicable diseases?
questions.correct_answer: Diseases that can spread from one person to another
questions.explanation: They are caused by pathogens such as bacteria, viruses, fungi, or worms.

questions.question: What are non-communicable diseases?
questions.correct_answer: Diseases that do not spread from person to person
questions.explanation: They are usually linked to lifestyle, diet, or environmental factors.

questions.question: What is immunity?
questions.correct_answer: The body's natural ability to fight against diseases
questions.explanation: The immune system protects the body from harmful pathogens.

questions.question: What is a vaccine?
questions.correct_answer: A substance that helps the body develop immunity against diseases
questions.explanation: Vaccines train the immune system to recognize and fight pathogens.

questions.question: What are antibiotics used for?
questions.explanation: Antibiotics kill bacteria or stop them from multiplying.

questions.question: Why should antibiotics not be used unnecessarily?
questions.correct_answer: Because misuse can lead to antibiotic resistance
questions.explanation: Antibiotic resistance makes bacteria harder to treat.

notes.title: Health
notes.content: Health includes physical, mental, and social well-being, not just the absence of disease.

notes.title: Healthy Lifestyle
notes.content: Eating nutritious food, exercising regularly, maintaining hygiene, and getting enough sleep help maintain good health.

notes.title: Communicable Diseases
notes.content: Communicable diseases spread through air, water, food, direct contact, or vectors such as mosquitoes.

notes.title: Vaccination
notes.content: Vaccines help prevent diseases by training the immune system to recognize pathogens.

notes.title: Antibiotics
notes.content: Antibiotics work only against bacterial infections and should be used only when prescribed by a doctor.

facts.fact: Health includes physical, mental, and social well-being.
Communicable diseases are caused by pathogens such as bacteria and viruses.
Vaccination has helped eliminate diseases like smallpox.
Antibiotics are ineffective against viral infections such as the common cold or flu.
